name: Ellavi Free-Flow Uterine Balloon Tamponade
description: The Ellavi Uterine Balloon Tamponade (UBT) is a minimally invasive, easy-to-use, and effective intervention for treating and managing severe postpartum bleeding.
keyFeatures: Design - The Ellavi uterine balloon tamponade comes with a supply bag has a 1000ml fluid capacity. The balloon is thin walled which ensures low expansion pressure.
Tubing - The Ellavi UBT comes with 2.25m tubing installed, this allows for vertical hydrostatic pressure control and rapid filling of the balloon.
T-valve Tap - The T-valve tap allows for the system to be either fixed or free-flow.
Effective Balloon - The UBT is equipped with a large enough, medical grade elastomer balloon (>750ml) to fill the entire uterus and apply equal pressure to the uterus wall once filled.

name: InnAccel Saans CPAP
description: The SAANS device universalizes access to breathing support for neonates and pediatric patients, thus ensuring that patients in ANY hospital or transport setting have access to life-saving respiratory therapy. The device can provide a range of therapy options and is designed to be usable even in settings with minimal infrastructure. The sensor-based system also brings superior usability and feedback, thus improving clinical patient care.
keyFeatures: Adaptability - The SAANS device is infrastructure independent. Its portability and ease-of-use ensures that it can be used efficiently in a hospital setting as well as in a transport setting. It can be used across multiple wards in the hospital; from NICU to labor to the ICU.

Efficiency - Within one device, one can achieve CPAP, HFNC and resuscitation Therapy for neonatal and pediatric patients. It is also Compatible with universal AC input with backup battery providing 4-6 hours of power.
Usability - The SAANS is compatible with multiple Oxygen sources: central line, cylinder and Oxygen concentrators. It also comes with an in-built flow generator and air-oxygen blending.
Displays and Alerts - It comes with alerts that signal changes in pressure, flow, FiO2, leakage and disconnection. It also has a large display and single control knob.

• Flow: 2 to 60 LPM
• Pressure (CPAP mode): 2 to 12 cm H2O
• PIP (Resuscitation mode): 5 to 40 cm H2O
• PEEP (Resuscitation mode): 2 to 10 H2O
• FiO2: 21% – 100%
• Separate modes for CPAP, HFNC, and Resuscitation delivery

• Digital control and display of PIP and PEEP (Resuscitation mode)
• Digital display of Pressure (CPAP mode)
• Integrated 8-inch LCD Display
• Parameter control with single touch knob
• External pressure control using PEEP valve or Bubble Jar options

name: LifeWrap Non-Pneumatic Anti-Shock Garment (NASG) – Size Medium/Large
description: The Non-Pneumatic Anti-Shock Garment (NASG) is a lightweight (1.5kg), washable and reusable first aid compression device made of neoprene fastened with Velcro over a foam compression ball. Once in place, the LifeWrap NASG applies enough circumferential counter-pressure to decrease blood loss and reverse shock by increasing blood pressure to the heart, brain, and lungs. In so doing, it reverses hypovolemic shock resulting from obstetric hemorrhage.
keyFeatures: Globally Trusted - The LifeWrap NASG has been delivered to dozens of countries with potential to be used on hundreds of thousands of women to manage complications with postpartum hemorrhage. The LifeWrap NASG has been widely delivered by VIA and used by providers throughout Africa, South Asia and Latin America, where maternal death rates remain high.

Usability - With minimal training, frontline health providers are able to successfully apply the garment to the bleeding patient. Once the mother’s bleeding is controlled, she can be safely transported to a hospital for obstetric care.
Reusable Quality - The LifeWrap NASG has been independently tested for up to 144 uses, putting the LifeWrap NASG at the forefront of quality and reusability.

Patient Accessibility - Importantly, the NASG does not need to be removed for uterine examinations or vaginal procedures, with the abdominal part only being opened for abdominal surgery. The patient’s vital signs often immediately improve upon application.

Transportable - LifeWrap NASG is lightweight and easily folded and packed, and once in use can stabilize patients for up to three days while patients are transported to a health center with the ability to administer blood transfusions and provide definitive medical or surgical interventions.

Expert Developed - The manufacturer works closely with the UCSF (University of California, San Francisco) and the Safe Motherhood Program and particularly with Dr. Suellen Miller, the leading global NASG expert and advocate of the LifeWrap NASG.

specifications.Product Dimensions:
• Tummy Total Width: 120 cm
• Hip Total Width: 96.5 cm
• Total Length: 123 cm

name: Liger Electrosurgical Generator
description: The ESU-110 Electrosurgical Generator is compact, portable, and battery powered. It includes an integrated smoke evacuation system and grounding pad which make it ideal for the LLETZ (LEEP) procedure for treating pre-cancerous cervical lesions. The ESU is very easy to use with a single button interface, which makes setup and completion of the procedure very simple.
keyFeatures: Portable - The total weight of the device with accessories is a combined 8lbs. This has allowed Dr’s to take the device to the most remote locations in the world.
CUT & COAG - Using a standard 3 prong electro surgical pencil you can perform both cut and coag functions.
Smoke Evacuation - The port at the front of the devices allows for a HEPA carbon filter and smoke evacuation tubing to be connected to a Graves Speculum.
Battery Operated - Closed loop DC circuit limits risk of accidental burns to patient from ground contact. No mains power infrastructure required for procedure.
Single button Interface - The device is specifically tuned for LEEP/LLETZ procedure. No power adjustments are necessary.

name: MTTS Beluga Resuscitator
description:
The Beluga Resuscitator has an innovative design that combines an oxygen blender and electronic PEEP/PIP setting into a single unit. Driven by battery, it can be used in the delivery units, NICUs and outside hospital settings in emergency situations.
Read our Buyer's Guides to learn more about Newborn Resuscitation Devices.
keyFeatures: Usability and Safety - Beluga combines a compact, quiet, and easy-to-operate technology with precise control of gas flow delivery and oxygen concentration. For added patient safety, Beluga incorporates a full complement of alarms.
Design - Beluga uses latest technology and together with user-friendly touch screen interface allows clinicians to save time and focus on the actual problems to solve.
Quality - Beluga resuscitator is a precise and reliable device that provides safe and effective therapy, high reliability components and long-life batteries.
Versatility - Beluga can be used for emergencies where quick action is required as well as in controlled hospital environment for prolonged period of time.

Effectiveness - Beluga resuscitator features electronic mixing for easy, independent adjustment of flow rate and FiO2. Beluga combines proven clinical effectiveness in respiratory care and low cost consumables with exceptional value.

name: MTTS Dolphin CPAP
description:
The MTTS Dolphin CPAP is a next generation bubble CPAP machine that combines the latest in respiratory care and patient monitoring technology with exceptional value. It is a comfortable alternative to relatively invasive therapies, endotracheal intubation and mechanical ventilation. The Dolphin CPAP system is designed to protect compromised airways, enhancing patient comfort and optimizing infant outcomes. Its continuous gentle pressure of air decreases the baby’s work of breathing by keeping the alveoli of the lungs open, enables efficient capillary exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide to take place, helps establish and maintain functional residual capacity, prevents collapse and upper airway obstruction, and reduces apnea, bradycardia and cyanotic episodes.
Read our Buyer's Guides to learn more about Bubble CPAPs.
keyFeatures: Humidifier - The Dolphin humidifier automatically refills for 3-days of continuous operation and has a small heated volume for quick warm up. The heater power is automatically adjusted to maintain correct humidity and minimize rain-out.

Patient Circuit - Composed of robust but flexible silicone tubes. Completely reusable and most parts are autoclavable. If an autoclave is not available, parts can be chemically disinfected. The circuit includes an internal heater wire and temperature sensors.

Pulse Oximeter - Pulse-oximetry is integrated into the device. The Dolphin uses Masimo SET® technology for accurate, reliable, and robust measurements. There is a simple user interface and alarm settings and no multi-level menus or complicated button sequences.

Air Compressor - The integrated air compressor means there is no need for an external air source. It is completely integrated, meaning no messy external wires or tubes and is a diaphragm type for clean, long lasting air supply.

name: MTTS Impala Ventilator
description:
The MTTS Impala Ventilator is an invasive mechanical ventilator for neonatal, pediatric and adult patients. It has 8 ventilation modes (ACVCV, AC-PCV, SIMV-VC, SIMV-PV, VCV/ PS, PCV/PS, CPAP, and BiPAP) and is intended for use in intensive care units and ambulances. The device contains a built-in pump, blender, flow, and pressure sensors that allows oxygen-rich (>21% O2) air with accurate fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2), pressure, and volume to be safely delivered to the patient.
Read our Buyer's Guides to learn more about Oxygen Therapy and Ventilation.
keyFeatures: Design - The Impala Ventilator is portable, which makes it suitable for use in emergencies or intensive care wards. Designed with a built-in battery for 4 hours of backup power. Further, the Impala has an easy-to-use touch screen and no time restriction on use due to the gas tank limitation.

Effective - The Impala Ventilator features electronic mixing for easy, independent adjustment of flow rate and FiO2. It also has ACV, SIMV and CPAP modes, and controlled target PIP and consistent PEEP.

Compatible - Impala combines a compact, quiet, and easy-to-operate technology with precise control of gas flow delivery and oxygen concentration. For added patient safety, Impala incorporates a full complement of alarms.
Cost Efficient - Impala combines proven clinical effectiveness in respiratory care and low cost consumables with exceptional value.

name: MTTS Lightmeter
description:
The MTTS Lightmeter provides precise measurement of the intensity of the blue light spectrum that is necessary to treat jaundice in infants. This measurement allows health professionals to ensure phototherapy units are working correctly and determine when a phototherapy device or its bulbs need to be replaced.
The Lightmeter was developed for use with the MTTS Firefly and MTTS Colibri Overhead Phototherapy, but it can be used for any phototherapy unit with LEDs, fluorescent bulbs or compact bulbs. When measuring a single-sided phototherapy machine, the Lightmeter will measure the light using the front sensor. When measuring a double-sided phototherapy, the MTTS Lightmeter reads the intensity from both sides, using the sensors on the front and back of the device. It then displays the sum of the values from each side.
keyFeatures: Quality Design - By measuring the wavelength spectrum from 400 to 500 nanometers, the Lightmeter gives a reliable irradiance measurement and helps to comply with the American Academy of Pediatrics Guidelines. Spectral response chosen to match wavelength used for Jaundice treatment. Two-sided measurement for convenient measurement of double-sided phototherapy devices such as MTTS Firefly.

Versatile - The Lightmeter can be used to measure the intensity of any type of phototherapy device used for the treatment of hyperbilirubinemia in newborn babies, including single-sided and double-sided machines. The Lightmeter can also be used as a tool for clinical decision making, allowing the care giver to determine how to position the baby, maximize light exposure, and provide the strongest phototherapy.
Displays/Controls - Reflective LCD screen can be read even under bright lights orion sunlight. Touch sensitive button cannot wear out or break.

name: Pumani bubbleCPAP
description:
The Pumani bubbleCPAP low-cost bubble CPAP respiratory device for newborns and children. This cost-effective CPAP is designed to run for years at a time and it includes spare parts and a repair manual for simple, annual maintenance. The Pumani provides the same therapeutic pressure as more complicated, gold-standard bCPAP devices in the United States. The Pumani consists of a flow source, a pressure source, and patient tubing. The flow comes from an air pump, and the pressure is provided by a water bottle. The only moving components are small diaphragms that cost less than $1 to replace and are included with the Pumani.
Pumani is currently used worldwide across 35 countries.
Read our Buyer's Guides to learn more about Bubble CPAPs.
keyFeatures: Usability - Clear labels on front panel of the Pumani allow for simple use. A new user can be trained to operate the Pumani in less than one day, and a user manual and repair manual are included with the system.

Adjustable - The Pumani is capable of delivering flow from 5 to 10 L/min and pressure from 5-8 cm H2O. Adjusting these settings is as easy as turning a knob or filling a water bottle. The device also has the ability to blend room air with oxygen from a concentrator or oxygen tank.
Controls - The Pumani is easy to operate thanks to a streamlined control panel design.

specifications.Power:
• Voltage: 220 Volts
• Max Power Consumption: 15 Watts

name: The IRIS by Liger: Colposcope and Coagulator in One
description: The IRIS from Liger Medical provides a comprehensive diagnostic and treatment solution for precancerous lesions. By combining a digital colposcope with a thermocoagulator, IRIS offers a full turnkey screen-and-treat program in a single sitting, addressing the critical issue of loss to follow-up in cervical cancer care. This system is the only battery-operated electrosurgical unit on the market, making it highly portable and effective even in low-resource settings. It features a comprehensive smoke evacuation system, minimizes the risk of accidental burns, and includes multiple audiovisual safety cues, ensuring patient safety and ease of use.
keyFeatures: Colposcope:

specifications.Transport and Storage:
• Ambient Temperature: 10° to 40° Celsius
• Relative Humidity: 0% to 80% non-condensing
specifications.Wireless Communication:
• Wi-Fi IEEE 802.11: ac/a/b/g/n
• Transmission: 2.40 to 2.48 GHz at 16dBm

testimonials.author: Smyta Joshi
testimonials.quote: The procedure went well. The 19 mm probe works well and I used it for 2-3 application as the cervix was bulky. The heating time, timer, etc. works well and cervix appears exactly how it appears after treatment with cold coagulation.

testimonials.author: Dr. Kofi Effah
testimonials.organization: Cervical Cancer Prevention and Training Centre, Catholic Hospital, Battor, Ghana
testimonials.quote: I took delivery of a package for our Cervical Cancer Prevention and Training Centre, Battor. Really light devices! Cervical cancer screening and treatment will never be the same again in Battor/Ghana! Simplified! Mobile services becoming more and more available. No need to carry large gas cylinders.

name: Thermocoagulator
description:
The Thermocoagulator emerges as a highly effective and mobile solution designed to address the critical need for accessible treatment options for cervical pre-cancer and various human tissue lesions through thermal ablation. This battery-operated, handheld device is characterized by its portability and ease of use, making it an ideal choice for both hospital settings and non-hospital rural healthcare environments where access to conventional power sources may be limited.

Adhering to the World Health Organization’s guidelines for combating the escalating incidence of cervical cancer, the Thermocoagulator is equipped with a selection of probes—two 19mm flat probes, one 19mm nipple probe, and one 16mm flat probe—to accommodate different treatment requirements. As a type B thermal coagulator, it is designed to deliver low-power, high-temperature destruction of precancerous cervical tissues or other lesions directly through contact with its heated probe tip. The device's reusability, combined with its non-sterile delivery, underscores its practicality and sustainability as a solution for reducing the prevalence of cervical cancer, particularly in regions where healthcare resources are scarce. The Thermocoagulator represents a pivotal advancement in the field of medical technology, offering a life-saving intervention that is both efficient and adaptable to the diverse needs of global healthcare.
Read our Buyer's Guides to learn more about Products for Cervical Cancer Diagnosis and Treatment.
keyFeatures: Heated Probe Tip - Contains a heating element, which is heated to 100°C (212°F) on the default setting for the destruction of human tissue. The probe tip should only be applied to tissue, which is intended to be ablated. The TC probe shaft is not intended to deliver heat. The probe shaft and sidewall tip has a maximum temperature of 43°C (109°F). Contact with the probe shaft should be avoided during the procedure.
Usability - Automated procedure timing with visual and audible indicators – no watch or clock required. Can be set to 20, 30, 40, or 60 seconds and 80, 90, 100, or 120°C.
Exchangeable Probes - Heat quickly (5-8 seconds) that can be autoclaved for sterilization.
Portable - Lightweight with no tether wires or cables required to connect probe to device and no external power supply required for treatment.

specifications.Dimensions and Weight:
• Width: 1.5 inches (4 cm)
• Height: 8 inches (20 cm)
• Depth: 2 inches (5 cm)
• Weight: 11 oz (240 g)
specifications.General Info:
• Type B Applied Parts
• IP21 Rating: Solid particle protection: Level 2 (>12.5mm), Liquid ingress protection: Level 1 (dripping water)
specifications.Operating Conditions:
• Ambient Temperature: 10° to 40° Celsius
• Relative Humidity: 0% to 80% non-condensing
specifications.Transport and Storage:
• Ambient Temperature: -40° to 70° Celsius
• Relative Humidity: 0% to 80% non-condensing

boxContents: 1x Thermocoagulator Main Unit – Including 1x HTU-110 Handle

testimonials.quote: The procedure went well. The 19 mm probe works well and I used it for 2-3 application as the cervix was bulky. The heating time, timer, etc. works well and cervix appears exactly how it appears after treatment with cold coagulation.

Cervical cancer screening and treatment will never be the same again in Battor/Ghana! Simplified! Mobile services becoming more and more available. No need to carry large gas cylinders.

testimonials.author: Beatrice Kabota
testimonials.quote: Thermal coagulator is doing so great and it’s a nice machine so to say and very simple to use and assemble. We have both WISAP, the original table top and handheld. But I love the TC unit because of its simplicity to use. I am a national trainer for cervical cancer screening and last month we had a training, where we had participant from 25 out of 28 district in Malawi. In treatment of pre-cancer lesion we oriented all participants on how to use thermal coagulator so that when they are choosing method for treatment they have options. We use both TC (Thermocoagulator) and WISAP (Cold Coagulator), one comment that come out was the TC Unit is very simple to use and not complicated. And many participants really liked it. About the New WISAP. They don’t have a flat probe which makes your probes good for more options during treatment.
